 {"id":"caam-7vg","title":"Implement TUI backup action","description":"# Task: Implement TUI backup action

## Context
Wire up the backup functionality to the TUI using the new text input dialog component.

## Implementation

### Trigger
- Press 'b' or 'B' when viewing a provider's profile list
- Only available when auth files exist for that provider

### Flow
1. User presses 'b' on a provider
2. Check if auth files exist for provider
   - If not: show error message "No auth files found for <provider>"
   - If yes: continue
3. Open text input dialog
   - Title: "Backup Current Auth"
   - Prompt: "Enter profile name (e.g., email address):"
   - Placeholder: "user@example.com"
4. User enters profile name, presses Enter
5. Validate input:
   - Not empty
   - No forbidden characters (/, \, etc.)
   - Warn if profile already exists (overwrite?)
6. Call vault.Backup(provider, profileName)
7. Handle result:
   - Success: show message "Backed up <provider> auth to '<profile>'"
   - Error: show error message
8. Refresh profile list

### Overwrite Confirmation
If profile already exists, show confirmation dialog:
- "Profile '<name>' already exists. Overwrite? (y/n)"
- Or use the dialog with Yes/No buttons

### Key Bindings
- Add 'b' to the help text
- Document in keyboard shortcuts section

## Code Changes

### internal/tui/update.go
```go
case "b", "B":
    if m.canBackup() {
        return m.showBackupDialog()
    }
```

### internal/tui/model.go
Add method:
```go
func (m Model) showBackupDialog() (Model, tea.Cmd) {
    // Open dialog for backup
}

func (m Model) executeBackup(profileName string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        err := m.vault.Backup(m.selectedProvider, profileName)
        // Return result message
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'b' key triggers backup dialog when on provider
- [ ] Dialog prompts for profile name
- [ ] Validation prevents empty/invalid names
- [ ] Existing profile triggers overwrite confirmation
- [ ] Successful backup shows confirmation message
- [ ] Failed backup shows error message
- [ ] Profile list refreshes after backup
- [ ] Help text updated with new keybinding

 {"id":"caam-82c","title":"Add force-unlock command","description":"## Task
Add 'caam unlock' command to forcibly remove locks.

## Usage
```bash
caam unlock codex work-1          # Unlock specific profile
caam unlock --stale               # Clean all stale locks
caam unlock codex work-1 --force  # Force unlock even if process running
```

## Safety
- Default: Only unlock if process not running
- --force: Unlock even if process appears running (dangerous)
- Warn before force unlock
- Consider sending SIGTERM to process before unlocking

## Implementation
- Check if lock is stale (PID not running)
- If stale, just remove lock file
- If --force, warn and remove anyway
- If not stale and no --force, error

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can unlock stale locks
- Force requires explicit flag
- Warning before dangerous operations","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.864750655-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam profile unlock command with stale lock detection and --force flag","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.879518279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.880805974-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"caam-83za","title":"Integration tests: AuthPool + SmartRunner + Prediction","description":"# Task: Integration tests: AuthPool + SmartRunner + Prediction

## Overview
Integration tests that verify the interaction between major components works correctly end-to-end.

## Why This Matters
Unit tests verify individual components. But bugs often hide in the INTEGRATION:
- AuthPool provides ready profiles to SmartRunner
- Prediction engine triggers alerts that feed into SmartRunner decisions
- Session tracker feeds burn rate to prediction engine

## Test Scenarios

### 1. AuthPool → SmartRunner Integration
```go
func TestAuthPoolSmartRunnerIntegration(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing AuthPool provides ready profiles to SmartRunner")
    
    // Setup
    pool := authpool.NewTestPool(t)
    pool.AddProfile("claude", "alice", authpool.Ready)
    pool.AddProfile("claude", "bob", authpool.Ready)
    
    runner := exec.NewSmartRunner(
        exec.WithAuthPool(pool),
    )
    
    // Simulate rate limit
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Simulating rate limit on alice")
    err := runner.SimulateRateLimit()
    
    // Verify handoff to bob
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Current profile: %s", runner.CurrentProfile())
    require.Equal(t, "bob", runner.CurrentProfile())
    
    // Verify alice marked as cooldown in pool
    status := pool.GetStatus("claude", "alice")
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Alice pool status: %v", status)
    require.Equal(t, authpool.Cooldown, status)
}
```

### 2. Prediction → Alert → SmartRunner Integration
```go
func TestPredictionAlertIntegration(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing prediction triggers proactive handoff")
    
    // Setup prediction engine with mock data showing imminent depletion
    predictor := prediction.NewTestEngine(t)
    predictor.SetPrediction("alice", &prediction.Prediction{
        CurrentPercent: 92,
        TimeToDepletion: 5 * time.Minute,
        Warning: prediction.WarningImminent,
    })
    
    alertChan := make(chan *notify.Alert, 10)
    runner := exec.NewSmartRunner(
        exec.WithPredictor(predictor),
        exec.WithAlertCallback(func(a *notify.Alert) {
            alertChan <- a
        }),
    )
    
    // Start runner
    runner.Start(ctx)
    
    // Verify alert generated
    select {
    case alert := <-alertChan:
        t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Alert received: %+v", alert)
        require.Contains(t, alert.Message, "5 minutes")
    case <-time.After(time.Second):
        t.Fatal("Expected alert not received")
    }
}
```

### 3. SessionTracker → BurnRate → Prediction Integration
```go
func TestSessionTrackerPredictionIntegration(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing live session data feeds prediction")
    
    tracker := usage.NewSessionTracker()
    predictor := prediction.NewEngine(
        prediction.WithSessionTracker(tracker),
    )
    
    // Simulate usage
    for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
        tracker.Record(usage.TokenEntry{
            Timestamp:    time.Now().Add(-time.Duration(i) * time.Minute),
            OutputTokens: 1000,
        })
    }
    
    // Get prediction
    pred, err := predictor.Predict(ctx, "claude", "alice")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Burn rate: %.2f tokens/min", pred.BurnRate.TokensPerMinute)
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Time to depletion: %v", pred.TimeToDepletion)
    
    // Verify burn rate is approximately 1000 tokens/min
    require.InDelta(t, 1000, pred.BurnRate.TokensPerMinute, 100)
}
```

### 4. Vault → Pool → Handoff → Vault Integration
```go
func TestVaultPoolHandoffCycle(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing full vault → pool → handoff cycle")
    
    // Setup real vault with test profiles
    vault := authfile.NewTestVault(t)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "alice", validAuthData)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "bob", validAuthData)
    
    pool := authpool.NewAuthPool(authpool.WithVault(vault))
    pool.MonitorOnce(ctx) // Populate pool state
    
    runner := exec.NewSmartRunner(
        exec.WithVault(vault),
        exec.WithAuthPool(pool),
    )
    
    // Verify initial state
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Initial profile: %s", vault.ActiveProfile("claude"))
    
    // Trigger handoff
    runner.SimulateRateLimit()
    
    // Verify vault state changed
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] After handoff: %s", vault.ActiveProfile("claude"))
    require.NotEqual(t, "alice", vault.ActiveProfile("claude"))
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Every test prefixed with [INTEGRATION]
- Log state before and after each operation
- Log timing of operations
- Log all component interactions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All integration paths tested
- [ ] No mocking of integration points (use real components)
- [ ] Clear logging of component interactions
- [ ] Tests run in <10 seconds
- [ ] Deterministic (no flaky tests)

 {"id":"caam-84lq","title":"Implement provider-specific login handlers","description":"# Task: Implement provider-specific login handlers

## Overview
Create login handlers for each provider that know how to trigger and complete the login flow via PTY injection.

## Background
Different providers have different login flows:
- Claude Code: /login → browser auth or device code
- Codex: Already running, may need codex login
- Gemini: /auth command

Each handler needs to know:
1. What command triggers login
2. What output indicates login in progress
3. What output indicates success
4. What output indicates failure

## Technical Details

### LoginHandler Interface
```go
// internal/handoff/handler.go

type LoginHandler interface {
    // Provider returns the provider ID this handler supports
    Provider() string
    
    // TriggerLogin injects the login command
    TriggerLogin(pty *pty.Controller) error
    
    // IsLoginInProgress returns true if login flow has started
    IsLoginInProgress(output string) bool
    
    // IsLoginComplete returns true if login succeeded
    IsLoginComplete(output string) bool
    
    // IsLoginFailed returns true if login failed, with error message
    IsLoginFailed(output string) (bool, string)
}
```

### Claude Handler
```go
type ClaudeLoginHandler struct{}

func (h *ClaudeLoginHandler) TriggerLogin(pty *pty.Controller) error {
    return pty.InjectCommand("/login")
}

func (h *ClaudeLoginHandler) IsLoginComplete(output string) bool {
    // Look for "Successfully logged in" or similar
    return strings.Contains(output, "logged in") ||
           strings.Contains(output, "authenticated")
}
```

### Handler Registry
```go
var handlers = map[string]LoginHandler{
    "claude": &ClaudeLoginHandler{},
    "codex":  &CodexLoginHandler{},
    "gemini": &GeminiLoginHandler{},
}

func GetHandler(provider string) LoginHandler {
    return handlers[provider]
}
```

- internal/handoff/claude.go
- internal/handoff/codex.go
- internal/handoff/gemini.go

 {"id":"caam-8y9","title":"Implement CLI sync commands","description":"# Task: Implement CLI sync commands

## Context
Implement the CLI commands for managing the sync pool and triggering synchronization.

## Commands

### `caam sync`
Sync now with all machines in pool.

```bash
caam sync [flags]

Flags:
  --machine NAME    Sync only with specific machine
  --provider PROV   Sync only specific provider
  --profile PROF    Sync only specific profile
  --dry-run         Show what would sync without doing it
  --force           Force sync even if recently synced
  --json            Output results as JSON
```

Output:
```
Syncing with 3 machines...

  work-laptop (192.168.1.100):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed (local fresher)
    ✓ claude/bob@gmail.com: pulled (remote fresher)
    ✓ codex/work@company.com: up to date
    
  home-desktop (10.0.0.50):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed
    ✓ gemini/personal@gmail.com: pulled
    
  cloud-vm (34.123.45.67):
    ✗ Connection failed: timeout

Sync complete: 4 pushed, 2 pulled, 1 up to date, 1 error
```

### `caam sync init` ⭐ NEW
Interactive first-time setup wizard:

```bash
caam sync init [flags]

Flags:
  --discover    Auto-discover from SSH config
  --csv         Create CSV template only
```

Flow:

### `caam sync add`
Add a machine to the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync add <name> <address> [flags]

Flags:
  --key PATH        Path to SSH private key (default: auto-detect)
  --user NAME       SSH username (default: current user)
  --remote-path P   Path to caam data on remote (default: same as local)
  --test            Test connectivity after adding
  --no-test         Skip connectivity test
```

Examples:
```bash
# Basic add (auto-detects SSH key)
caam sync add work-laptop 192.168.1.100

# With specific key and test
caam sync add cloud-vm 34.123.45.67 --key ~/.ssh/cloud_key --test

# With port and user in address
caam sync add dev-server jeff@dev.example.com:2222
```

### `caam sync remove`
Remove a machine from the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync remove <name> [flags]

Flags:
  --force    Skip confirmation
```

### `caam sync test`
Test connectivity to one or all machines.

```bash
caam sync test [name] [flags]

Flags:
  --all     Test all machines in pool
```

 {"id":"caam-npy","title":"Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm

## Context
The core logic that determines which token is "fresher" and should be synchronized. This is the brain of the sync system.

## Token Freshness

### What Makes a Token Fresh?
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. A "fresher" token:
1. Has a later expiry time (primary criterion)
2. Was created/modified more recently (tiebreaker)

### Token Structure Analysis

#### Claude Token (`.claude.json`)
```json
{
  "oauthToken": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauthToken.expiry`

#### Codex Token (`auth.json`)
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734623340
}
```
Key field: `expires_at` (Unix timestamp)

#### Gemini Token (`settings.json`)
```json
{
  "oauth_credentials": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauth_credentials.expiry`

### Freshness Extractor
```go
package sync

// TokenFreshness represents the freshness of a token
type TokenFreshness struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    ModifiedAt  time.Time
    IsExpired   bool
    Source      string // "local" or machine name
}

// ExtractFreshness parses auth files to determine token freshness
func ExtractFreshness(provider, profile string, authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)

// CompareFreshness returns true if a is fresher than b
func CompareFreshness(a, b *TokenFreshness) bool {
    // Primary: later expiry wins
    if !a.ExpiresAt.Equal(b.ExpiresAt) {
        return a.ExpiresAt.After(b.ExpiresAt)
    }
    // Tiebreaker: later modification wins
    return a.ModifiedAt.After(b.ModifiedAt)
}
```

### Provider-Specific Extractors
```go
type FreshnessExtractor interface {
    Extract(authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)
}

func GetExtractor(provider string) FreshnessExtractor {
    switch provider {
    case "claude":
        return &ClaudeFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "codex":
        return &CodexFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "gemini":
        return &GeminiFreshnessExtractor{}
    }
}
```

## Sync Algorithm

### Sync Operation
```go
type SyncOperation struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    Direction   string    // "push" or "pull"
    Machine     *Machine
    LocalFresh  *TokenFreshness
    RemoteFresh *TokenFreshness
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Operation   *SyncOperation
    Success     bool
    BytesSent   int64
    BytesRecv   int64
    Duration    time.Duration
    Error       error
}
```

### Full Sync Algorithm
```go
func (s *Syncer) SyncWithMachine(m *Machine) ([]*SyncResult, error) {
    results := []*SyncResult{}
    
    // 1. Connect to remote
    client, err := s.pool.Get(m)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("connection failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer s.pool.Release(m.ID)
    
    // 2. Get local profiles
    localProfiles := s.vault.ListAllProfiles()
    
    // 3. Get remote profiles
    remoteProfiles, err := s.listRemoteProfiles(client)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("list remote profiles: %w", err)
    }
    
    // 4. Merge profile lists (union)
    allProfiles := mergeProfileLists(localProfiles, remoteProfiles)
    
    // 5. For each profile, compare and sync
    for _, p := range allProfiles {
        op := s.determineSyncOperation(client, p)
        if op == nil {
            continue // Already in sync
        }
        
        result := s.executeOperation(client, op)
        results = append(results, result)
    }
    
    return results, nil
}
```

### Determine Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) determineSyncOperation(client *SSHClient, p ProfileRef) *SyncOperation {
    localFresh := s.getLocalFreshness(p)
    remoteFresh := s.getRemoteFreshness(client, p)
    
    switch {
    case localFresh == nil && remoteFresh != nil:
        // Only exists on remote: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    case localFresh != nil && remoteFresh == nil:
        // Only exists locally: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(localFresh, remoteFresh):
        // Local is fresher: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(remoteFresh, localFresh):
        // Remote is fresher: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    default:
        // Equal freshness: no action
        return nil
    }
}
```

### Execute Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) executeOperation(client *SSHClient, op *SyncOperation) *SyncResult {
    start := time.Now()
    
    switch op.Direction {
    case "push":
        // Read local files
        files, err := s.vault.ReadProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to remote
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        err = client.BatchWrite(remotePathFiles(remotePath, files))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
    case "pull":
        // Read remote files
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        files, err := client.BatchRead(remoteFilePaths(remotePath))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to local vault
        err = s.vault.WriteProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile, files)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
    }
    
    return &SyncResult{
        Success:  true,
        Duration: time.Since(start),
        ...
    }
}
```

## Conflict Resolution

### Simultaneous Updates
If two machines update the same profile at nearly the same time:
- Both will detect their local as "fresher"
- Both will push
- Result: last write wins (deterministic by timestamp)

This is acceptable for v1. Future enhancement: conflict detection and resolution UI.

### Expired Tokens
Even expired tokens might be useful (for refresh_token). Don't skip expired tokens in sync.

+{"id":"caam-ogpo","title":"Performance benchmarks for critical sub-100ms paths","description":"# Task: Performance benchmarks for critical sub-100ms paths

## Overview
Benchmark tests to ensure critical operations meet the sub-100ms latency target that makes caam feel instant.

## Why This Matters
caam differentiates by being FAST:
- Profile switching: <50ms (not 30 seconds with browser)
- Auth file swap: <10ms
- Rate limit detection: <1ms per line
- PTY injection: <5ms

If these are slow, caam loses its value proposition.

## Benchmarks

### 1. Auth File Swap
```go
func BenchmarkAuthFileSwap(b *testing.B) {
    vault := authfile.NewTestVault(b)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "alice", largeAuthData)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "bob", largeAuthData)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        profile := []string{"alice", "bob"}[i%2]
        vault.SwapActive(profile)
    }
    
    // Target: <10ms per swap
    // Report: b.ReportMetric(float64(elapsed)/float64(b.N), "ns/swap")
}
```

### 2. Rate Limit Detection
```go
func BenchmarkRateLimitDetection(b *testing.B) {
    detector := ratelimit.NewDetector(ratelimit.DefaultPatterns())
    lines := loadTestLines(1000) // Mix of normal and rate limit lines
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        for _, line := range lines {
            detector.Check(line)
        }
    }
    
    // Target: <1ms per 1000 lines
}
```

### 3. PTY Command Injection
```go
func BenchmarkPTYInjection(b *testing.B) {
    ctrl := pty.NewTestController(b)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        ctrl.InjectCommand("/login")
    }
    
    // Target: <5ms per injection
}
```

### 4. JWT Parsing
```go
func BenchmarkJWTParsing(b *testing.B) {
    token := loadTestJWT() // Large JWT with many claims
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        identity.ExtractFromJWT(token)
    }
    
    // Target: <1ms per parse
}
```

### 5. Log Parsing (per file)
```go
func BenchmarkLogParsing(b *testing.B) {
    logFile := createTestLogFile(10000) // 10k entries
    scanner := logs.NewClaudeScanner()
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        scanner.ScanFile(ctx, logFile, time.Time{})
    }
    
    // Target: <100ms per 10k entries
}
```

### 6. Burn Rate Calculation
```go
func BenchmarkBurnRateCalc(b *testing.B) {
    entries := generateTestEntries(1000)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        usage.CalculateBurnRate(entries, 2*time.Hour)
    }
    
    // Target: <10ms per calculation
}
```

### 7. Full Handoff Latency (E2E)
```go
func BenchmarkHandoffLatency(b *testing.B) {
    runner := setupTestRunner(b)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        start := time.Now()
        runner.SimulateRateLimitAndHandoff()
        elapsed := time.Since(start)
        
        if elapsed > 5*time.Second {
            b.Errorf("Handoff too slow: %v", elapsed)
        }
    }
    
    // Target: <5 seconds total (including login wait)
}
```

## Overview
Create a unified notification system that can deliver alerts through multiple channels: terminal output, desktop notifications, and webhooks.

Different users have different workflows:
- Terminal warriors: Want inline notifications in their session
- Multi-monitor users: Want desktop popups they cant miss
- DevOps teams: Want webhook integration with Slack/Discord
- Automation: Want machine-readable alerts for scripts

## Technical Details

### Notifier Interface
```go
// internal/notify/notify.go

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(alert *Alert) error
    Name() string
    Available() bool  // Check if this notifier works on this system
}

type Alert struct {
    Level     AlertLevel  // Info, Warning, Critical
    Title     string
    Message   string
    Profile   string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Action    string      // Suggested action
}
```

### Terminal Notifier
```go
type TerminalNotifier struct {
    writer io.Writer  // Usually os.Stderr
    color  bool
}

func (n *TerminalNotifier) Notify(alert *Alert) error {
    // Print colored alert to terminal
    // Format: [WARN] claude/alice@gmail.com: 85% usage, switch recommended
}
```

### Desktop Notifier
```go
type DesktopNotifier struct{}

func (n *DesktopNotifier) Notify(alert *Alert) error {
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "linux":
        // Use notify-send (libnotify)
        return exec.Command("notify-send", "-u", urgency, alert.Title, alert.Message).Run()
    case "darwin":
        // Use osascript
        script := fmt.Sprintf(`display notification "%s" with title "%s"`, alert.Message, alert.Title)
        return exec.Command("osascript", "-e", script).Run()
    case "windows":
        // Use PowerShell toast or BurntToast module
        // Or gracefully skip
    }
}

func (n *DesktopNotifier) Available() bool {
    // Check if notify-send/osascript exists
}
```

### Webhook Notifier
```go
type WebhookNotifier struct {
    URL     string
    Timeout time.Duration
    Headers map[string]string
}

func (n *WebhookNotifier) Notify(alert *Alert) error {
    payload := map[string]interface{}{
        "level":     alert.Level.String(),
        "title":     alert.Title,
        "message":   alert.Message,
        "profile":   alert.Profile,
        "timestamp": alert.Timestamp.Format(time.RFC3339),
    }
    // POST to webhook URL
}
```

### Multi-Notifier
```go
type MultiNotifier struct {
    notifiers []Notifier
}

func (m *MultiNotifier) Notify(alert *Alert) error {
    var errs []error
    for _, n := range m.notifiers {
        if n.Available() {
            if err := n.Notify(alert); err != nil {
                errs = append(errs, err)
            }
        }
    }
    return errors.Join(errs...)
}
```

+{"id":"caam-t1p8","title":"Implement depletion prediction engine","description":"# Task: Implement depletion prediction engine

## Overview
Create the prediction engine that combines current usage, burn rate, and historical patterns to forecast when rate limits will be hit.

## Technical Details

### Prediction Engine
```go
// internal/prediction/engine.go

type PredictionEngine struct {
    logScanner     logs.Scanner
    usageFetcher   *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    sessionTracker *usage.SessionTracker  // NEW: Real-time session data
}

type Prediction struct {
    Profile         string
    CurrentPercent  float64
    BurnRate        *usage.BurnRateInfo
    PredictedTime   time.Time
    TimeToDepletion time.Duration
    Confidence      float64
    Warning         WarningLevel
    DataSources     []string  // "logs", "session", "api"
}

type WarningLevel int
const (
    WarningNone WarningLevel = iota
    WarningApproaching  // < 30 min
    WarningImminent     // < 10 min
)

func (e *PredictionEngine) Predict(ctx context.Context, provider, profile string) (*Prediction, error) {
    // 1. Fetch current usage from API
    usageInfo, err := e.usageFetcher.Fetch(ctx, provider, profile)
    
    // 2. Get burn rate from multiple sources (prioritize most recent)
    var burnRate *usage.BurnRateInfo
    var sources []string
    
    // 2a. Check real-time session data first (most accurate)
    if e.sessionTracker != nil {
        if sessionRate := e.sessionTracker.BurnRate(30 * time.Minute); sessionRate != nil {
            burnRate = sessionRate
            sources = append(sources, "session")
        }
    }
    
    // 2b. Fall back to log data if no session data
    if burnRate == nil {
        if logEntries, err := e.logScanner.Scan(ctx, "", 2*time.Hour); err == nil {
            burnRate = usage.CalculateBurnRate(logEntries.Entries, 2*time.Hour)
            sources = append(sources, "logs")
        }
    }
    
    // 2c. Fall back to API-reported rate if available
    if burnRate == nil && usageInfo.BurnRate != nil {
        burnRate = usageInfo.BurnRate
        sources = append(sources, "api")
    }
    
    // 3. Calculate time to depletion
    var predictedTime time.Time
    var timeToDepletion time.Duration
    if burnRate != nil && burnRate.PercentPerHour > 0 {
        remainingPercent := 100.0 - usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.UsedPercent
        hoursUntilDepletion := remainingPercent / burnRate.PercentPerHour
        timeToDepletion = time.Duration(hoursUntilDepletion * float64(time.Hour))
        predictedTime = time.Now().Add(timeToDepletion)
        
        // Cap at window reset time
        if usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.ResetsAt.Before(predictedTime) {
            predictedTime = usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.ResetsAt
            timeToDepletion = time.Until(predictedTime)
        }
    }
    
    // 4. Determine warning level
    warning := WarningNone
    if timeToDepletion > 0 && timeToDepletion < 10*time.Minute {
        warning = WarningImminent
    } else if timeToDepletion > 0 && timeToDepletion < 30*time.Minute {
        warning = WarningApproaching
    }
    
    // 5. Calculate confidence
    confidence := calculateConfidence(burnRate, len(sources))
    
    return &Prediction{
        Profile:         profile,
        CurrentPercent:  usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.UsedPercent,
        BurnRate:        burnRate,
        PredictedTime:   predictedTime,
        TimeToDepletion: timeToDepletion,
        Confidence:      confidence,
        Warning:         warning,
        DataSources:     sources,
    }, nil
}

func calculateConfidence(burnRate *usage.BurnRateInfo, sourceCount int) float64 {
    if burnRate == nil {
        return 0
    }
    
    // Base confidence from burn rate calculation
    confidence := burnRate.Confidence
    
    // Boost for multiple data sources
    if sourceCount > 1 {
        confidence = math.Min(1.0, confidence * 1.2)
    }
    
    return confidence
}
```

## Data Source Priority
1. **Real-time session data** (most accurate, most recent)
2. **Historical log data** (good accuracy, but may be stale)
3. **API-reported rate** (if provider exposes it)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Uses session tracker when available
- [ ] Falls back to log data gracefully
- [ ] Calculates accurate time to depletion
- [ ] Confidence reflects data quality
- [ ] Warning levels trigger at correct thresholds
- [ ] Handles edge cases (no data, window reset)

## Test Cases
1. Predict with session data only
2. Predict with log data only
3. Predict with multiple data sources
4. Predict when approaching window reset
5. Low confidence when data is sparse

## Dependencies
- Create BurnRateInfo struct and calculation (caam-ef8r)
- Add depletion prediction to UsageInfo (caam-3vsd)
- Implement real-time session tracking (caam-blxo)

- internal/prediction/engine.go
- internal/prediction/confidence.go
- internal/prediction/engine_test.go

Workspace presets that switch all tools atomically.

```bash

  --claude=work-claude \
  --codex=work-codex \
  --gemini=work-gemini

  --claude=personal \

current: work
```

 {"id":"caam-thb","title":"Token expiry parsing for all providers","description":"# Token Expiry Parsing

## Purpose
Parse token expiration timestamps from each provider authentication files to determine how long tokens remain valid.

## Background
Each provider stores auth differently. We need to extract expiry info from:
1. Claude Code - oauth.json with expiry field
2. OpenAI Codex - auth.json with different structure
3. Google Gemini - gcloud ADC format

## Technical Requirements

### Parser Interface
```go
type ExpiryParser interface {
    ParseExpiry(authFilePath string) (time.Time, error)
}

func ParseClaudeExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
func ParseCodexExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
func ParseGeminiExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
```

### Provider-Specific Formats

#### Claude Code
```json
// ~/.claude.ai/oauth.json (example, needs verification)
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": "2025-12-17T18:00:00Z",
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```
Alternative: `expires_in` (seconds) + issued time

#### OpenAI Codex
```json
// ~/.codex/auth.json (example, needs verification)
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734451200,  // Unix timestamp
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```

#### Google Gemini (ADC)
```json
// ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json
{
  "client_id": "...",
  "client_secret": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "type": "authorized_user"
}
```
Note: ADC may not store expiry - needs refresh to get expiry.

### Fallback Strategy
If expiry cannot be determined:
1. Check if refresh_token exists → assume valid but unknown expiry
2. No refresh_token → mark as "needs login"
3. Return sentinel value for "unknown expiry"

## Implementation Notes
- Study actual auth file formats (may need reverse engineering)
- Handle missing files gracefully
- Handle malformed JSON
- Support both Unix timestamps and ISO8601

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ParseClaudeExpiry implemented and tested
- [ ] ParseCodexExpiry implemented and tested  
- [ ] ParseGeminiExpiry implemented and tested
- [ ] Handles missing files (returns error)
- [ ] Handles malformed JSON (returns error)
- [ ] Unit tests with fixture files

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/expiry.go` (new)
- `internal/health/expiry_test.go` (new)
- `internal/health/testdata/` (fixtures)

## Research Needed
- [ ] Verify Claude Code auth file format
- [ ] Verify Codex auth file format
- [ ] Determine if Gemini ADC includes expiry
